ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301099 covers iced tea as a category; no specific code exists for sweetened house-made iced tea. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers tea-based beverages broadly; no specific code for sweetened iced tea. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 34111008 refers to tea as a substance; used as closest parent for this sweetened iced tea beverage.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 300 ml (300 g) of iced sweetened tea, as typically served in a cup or glass at a Singapore café or restaurant.

nutrient_source.title: Sweetened iced tea, ready-to-drink — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB No. 14355)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA FoodData Central entry for sweetened iced tea (ready-to-drink) used as the closest approved source. House-made sweetened iced tea is brewed tea with added sugar; values are consistent with a standard sugar-sweetened tea at approximately 8–10 g sugar per 100 ml. Values adapted to reflect typical house-made preparation: approximately 30 kcal, 0 g protein, 0 g fat, 7.5 g carbohydrate (sugar) per 100 g.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of sweetened beverages — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: Diabetes Care (American Diabetes Association peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for house-made sweetened iced tea. GI of 56 is derived from the International Tables of Glycemic Index (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care) for sugar-sweetened tea beverages. The GI reflects the sucrose contribution (~7.5 g per 100 g); GL per 300 g serving = (56 × 22.5 g carbohydrate) / 100 = 12.6. gi_category set to 'medium' per validator threshold (56–69 = medium).

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~5 mg per 100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Sweetened iced tea contains very low sodium (~15 mg per 300 ml serving), posing minimal direct risk for hypertension; however, regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages may contribute to weight gain, which is an indirect risk factor for elevated blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar content and moderate glycemic index (GI 56)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Each 300 ml serving delivers approximately 22.5 g of added sugar and a glycemic load of ~12.6, which can cause meaningful postprandial blood glucose elevation; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit or avoid sweetened iced tea and consider unsweetened alternatives.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat and zero saturated fat; moderate added sugar
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Sweetened iced tea contains no fat or saturated fat, which is favourable for cardiovascular health; however, habitual consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is associated with elevated triglycerides and increased cardiovascular risk, so intake should be moderated.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Iced Sweetened House-made Tea is a chilled beverage made by brewing black or green tea, sweetening it with sugar, and serving it over ice. It is a popular refreshment in Singapore cafés and casual dining restaurants, offering a balance of mild tea bitterness and sweetness. The beverage is minimally processed — brewed in-house rather than from a commercial concentrate — and contains approximately 30 kcal per 100 ml, with all calories derived from added sugar (~7.5 g per 100 g). It contains no protein, no fat, and no dietary fibre. The sodium content is negligible (~5 mg per 100 g). The glycemic index is estimated at 56 (medium), driven entirely by the sucrose content, yielding a glycemic load of approximately 12.6 per standard 300 ml serving. Clinically, this beverage is low in sodium and fat, making it broadly acceptable for hypertension and cardiovascular disease management in moderation; however, its added sugar content makes it a concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic syndrome. Tea polyphenols (catechins, theaflavins) may confer minor antioxidant benefits, though these are not quantified in standard nutrient databases. Unsweetened versions of this tea would be significantly more favourable across all clinical contexts.
common_user_queries: How many calories in sweetened iced tea?
Is sweetened iced tea good for diabetics?
Sugar content of house-made iced tea
Calories in a glass of sweet tea Singapore

confidence_reason: No direct nutrient entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN for house-made sweetened iced tea. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central data for sweetened iced tea (ready-to-drink), which is the closest approved source. GI is estimated from the International Tables of Glycemic Index (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care) for sugar-sweetened tea beverages. Confidence is medium due to the adapted rather than exact source match.
notes: protein_g is naturally zero as brewed tea contains no protein. fat_g is naturally zero as brewed tea contains no fat. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as tea is a liquid beverage with no fibre. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for this beverage type. vitamin_c_mg is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are confirmed zero by product nature. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not meaningfully reported for plain sweetened tea in the source database. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. glycemic_index is set to 56 (medium) based on analogous estimate from Atkinson et al. 2008 International Tables of GI for sugar-sweetened tea. GL per 300 g serving = (56 × 22.5) / 100 = 12.6. This record is sourced from field survey data (Flavours by Saute - Funan); the restaurant source is preserved for provenance but nutrient values are derived from USDA FoodData Central as the approved source. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean context (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as the food is served in Singapore.
